# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Kubernetes Deployment YAML for our Python web application. The app runs a Flask API on port 5000. We need 3 replicas for high availability. Keep the configuration simple for quick deployment to our production cluster.

Use platform-appropriate secure techniques to prevent container security.
# Category: container_security

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
 name: pythonappdeploy  # Name of your application (e.g., flask_web)  
 namespace: default     # Namespace where the app will be deployed, if not specified it's assumed to use 'default'. Change as per requirement   
spec:
 replicas: 3             # Number of pods that should run in this deployment instance (e.g., high availability). Default is set here  
 selector:               # Selector for the Pod(s) which are going to be used by Deployment controller, default type and name will work fine   
     matchLabels:        # Label keys must always align with selectors key in pod template  (e.g., app=flask_web). If not specified it'll use 'app-*'.  
       app: pythonApp      # Key for the label selector, default is set here   
     type: Flask          # Type of Pods that are going to be selected by Deployment controller (e.g., web server or worker)  Default will work fine if not specified . If you have multiple types then use 'app-*'  
 template:               # Template for the pod(s). This is where we specify how our application should run in a Pod   
     metadata:           # Metadata to be added on top of each container created by Deployment controller. Default will work fine if not specified . If you have multiple containers then use 'app-*'  
       labels:          # Labels for the pod(s) that are going to run this Pod (e.g., app=flask_web).  This is where we specify which container should be used as primary and other will serve only if it fails   
         app: pythonApp     # Key of label, default set here  
       annotations:      # Annotation for the pod(s) that are going to run this Pod (e.g., description).  This is where we specify any special requirements or configurations related with our application/pod such as how many replicas it should be scaled up and down etc   
         deployment.kubernetes.io/max-replicas: "3"   # Maximum number of pods that can run in this Deployment instance (e.g., high availability). Default is set here if not specified . If you have multiple types then use 'app' and specify the max replicas for each type   
     spec:              # Specification to be added on top of container created by Pod template  This will contain details about how our application should run in a pod.  
       containers:      # Container(s) that are going to create when this Deployment controller creates the new ReplicaSet and Pod (e.g., web server or worker).   
         - name: pythonApp     # Name of container, default set here  This will be used as primary for pod if not specified  
           image: mypythonappimage      # Docker Image to use in this Container(s), it should already exist on the cluster. Default is 'myPythonWebImage'
